name: postgrest

metadata.name: postgrest
metadata.summary: PostgREST client for Python. This library provides an ORM interface to PostgREST.

documentation:
# postgrest-py

[PostgREST](https://postgrest.org) client for Python. This library provides an "ORM-like" interface to PostgREST.

## INSTALLATION

### Requirements

- Python >= 3.9
- PostgreSQL >= 13
- PostgREST >= 11

### Local PostgREST server

If you want to use a local PostgREST server for development, you can use our preconfigured instance via Docker Compose.

```sh
docker-compose up
```

Once Docker Compose started, PostgREST is accessible at <http://localhost:3000>.

### Instructions

#### With Poetry (recommended)

```sh
poetry add postgrest
```

#### With Pip

```sh
pip install postgrest
```

## USAGE

### Getting started

```py
import asyncio
from postgrest import AsyncPostgrestClient

async def main():
    async with AsyncPostgrestClient("http://localhost:3000") as client:
        r = await client.from_("countries").select("*").execute()
        countries = r.data

asyncio.run(main())
```

### Create

```py
await client.from_("countries").insert({ "name": "Việt Nam", "capital": "Hà Nội" }).execute()
```

### Read

```py
r = await client.from_("countries").select("id", "name").execute()
countries = r.data
```

### Update

```py
await client.from_("countries").update({"capital": "Hà Nội"}).eq("name", "Việt Nam").execute()
```

### Delete

```py
await client.from_("countries").delete().eq("name", "Việt Nam").execute()
```

### General filters

### Stored procedures (RPC)
```py
await client.rpc("foobar", {"arg1": "value1", "arg2": "value2"}).execute()
```

## DEVELOPMENT

```sh
git clone https://github.com/supabase/postgrest-py.git
cd postgrest-py
poetry install
poetry run pre-commit install
```

### Testing

```sh
poetry run pytest
```

## CHANGELOG

Read more [here](https://github.com/supabase/postgrest-py/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md).

## SPONSORS

We are building the features of Firebase using enterprise-grade, open source products. We support existing communities wherever possible, and if the products don’t exist we build them and open source them ourselves. Thanks to these sponsors who are making the OSS ecosystem better for everyone.

methods.name: postgrest._sync.client.SyncPostgrestClient.rpc
documentation:
Perform a stored procedure call.

Args:

: func: The name of the remote procedure to run.
  params: The parameters to be passed to the remote procedure.
  count: The method to use to get the count of rows returned.
  head: When set to `true`, `data` will not be returned. Useful if you only need the count.
  get: When set to `true`, the function will be called with read-only access mode.

Returns:

: {class}`AsyncRPCFilterRequestBuilder`

Example:
: ```python
  await client.rpc("foobar", {"arg": "value"}).execute()
  ```

:::{versionchanged} 0.10.9 This method now returns a :class:`AsyncRPCFilterRequestBuilder`.
:::

:::{versionchanged} 0.10.2 This method now returns a :class:`AsyncFilterRequestBuilder` which allows you to filter on the RPC's resultset.
:::
PostgREST client.

classes.name: postgrest.base_client.BasePostgrestClient

methods.name: postgrest.base_client.BasePostgrestClient.auth
documentation:
Authenticate the client with either bearer token or basic authentication.

Raises:

: `ValueError`: If neither authentication scheme is provided.

:::{note}
Bearer token is preferred if both ones are provided.
:::

methods.name: postgrest.base_client.BasePostgrestClient.schema
documentation: Switch to another schema.
Base PostgREST client.

methods.name: postgrest._sync.request_builder.SyncQueryRequestBuilder.execute
documentation:
Execute the query.

:::{tip}
This is the last method called, after the query is built.
:::

Returns:

: {class}`APIResponse`

Raises:

: {class}`APIError` If the API raised an error.

methods.name: postgrest._sync.request_builder.SyncSingleRequestBuilder.execute
documentation:
Execute the query.

:::{tip}
This is the last method called, after the query is built.
:::

Returns:

: {class}`SingleAPIResponse`

Raises:

: {class}`APIError` If the API raised an error.

classes.name: postgrest._sync.request_builder.SyncSelectRequestBuilder

methods.name: postgrest._sync.request_builder.SyncSelectRequestBuilder.single
documentation:
Specify that the query will only return a single row in response.

:::{caution}
The API will raise an error if the query returned more than one row.
:::

methods.name: postgrest._sync.request_builder.SyncSelectRequestBuilder.maybe_single
documentation: Retrieves at most one row from the result. Result must be at most one row (e.g. using `eq` on a UNIQUE column), otherwise this will result in an error.

methods.name: postgrest._sync.request_builder.SyncSelectRequestBuilder.csv
documentation: Specify that the query must retrieve data as a single CSV string.
ignoring type checking as a workaround for https://github.com/python/mypy/issues/9319

methods.name: postgrest._sync.request_builder.SyncRequestBuilder.select
documentation:
Run a SELECT query.

Args:

: columns: The names of the columns to fetch.
  count: The method to use to get the count of rows returned.

Returns:

: {class}`SyncSelectRequestBuilder`

methods.name: postgrest._sync.request_builder.SyncRequestBuilder.insert
documentation:
Run an INSERT query.

Args:

: json: The row to be inserted.
  count: The method to use to get the count of rows returned.
  returning: Either 'minimal' or 'representation'
  upsert: Whether the query should be an upsert.
  default_to_null: Make missing fields default to `null`.

  > Otherwise, use the default value for the column.
  > Only applies for bulk inserts.

Returns:

: {class}`SyncQueryRequestBuilder`

methods.name: postgrest._sync.request_builder.SyncRequestBuilder.upsert
documentation:
Run an upsert (INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE) query.

Args:

: json: The row to be inserted.
  count: The method to use to get the count of rows returned.
  returning: Either 'minimal' or 'representation'
  ignore_duplicates: Whether duplicate rows should be ignored.
  on_conflict: Specified columns to be made to work with UNIQUE constraint.
  default_to_null: Make missing fields default to `null`. Otherwise, use the

  > default value for the column. This only applies when inserting new rows,
  > not when merging with existing rows under `ignoreDuplicates: false`.
  > This also only applies when doing bulk upserts.

Returns:

: {class}`SyncQueryRequestBuilder`

methods.name: postgrest._sync.request_builder.SyncRequestBuilder.update
documentation:
Run an UPDATE query.

Args:

: json: The updated fields.
  count: The method to use to get the count of rows returned.
  returning: Either 'minimal' or 'representation'

Returns:

: {class}`SyncFilterRequestBuilder`

methods.name: postgrest._sync.request_builder.SyncRequestBuilder.delete
documentation:
Run a DELETE query.

Args:

: count: The method to use to get the count of rows returned.
  returning: Either 'minimal' or 'representation'

Returns:

: {class}`SyncFilterRequestBuilder`

classes.name: postgrest.base_request_builder.APIResponse

class_variables.name: postgrest.base_request_builder.APIResponse.data
documentation: The data returned by the query.

class_variables.name: postgrest.base_request_builder.APIResponse.count
documentation: The number of rows returned.
the APIResponse.data is marked as _ReturnT instead of list[_ReturnT]
as it is also returned in the case of rpc() calls; and rpc calls do not
necessarily return lists.
https://github.com/supabase-community/postgrest-py/issues/200

class_variables.name: postgrest.base_request_builder.SingleAPIResponse.data
documentation: The data returned by the query.
type: ignore

methods.name: postgrest.base_request_builder.BaseFilterRequestBuilder.not_
documentation: Whether the filter applied next should be negated.

methods.name: postgrest.base_request_builder.BaseFilterRequestBuilder.filter
documentation:
Apply filters on a query.

Args:
    column: The name of the column to apply a filter on
    operator: The operator to use while filtering
    criteria: The value to filter by

methods.name: postgrest.base_request_builder.BaseFilterRequestBuilder.eq
documentation:
An 'equal to' filter.

Args:
    column: The name of the column to apply a filter on
    value: The value to filter by

methods.name: postgrest.base_request_builder.BaseFilterRequestBuilder.neq
documentation:
A 'not equal to' filter

Args:
    column: The name of the column to apply a filter on
    value: The value to filter by

methods.name: postgrest.base_request_builder.BaseFilterRequestBuilder.gt
documentation:
A 'greater than' filter

Args:
    column: The name of the column to apply a filter on
    value: The value to filter by

methods.name: postgrest.base_request_builder.BaseFilterRequestBuilder.gte
documentation:
A 'greater than or equal to' filter

Args:
    column: The name of the column to apply a filter on
    value: The value to filter by

methods.name: postgrest.base_request_builder.BaseFilterRequestBuilder.lt
documentation:
A 'less than' filter

Args:
    column: The name of the column to apply a filter on
    value: The value to filter by

methods.name: postgrest.base_request_builder.BaseFilterRequestBuilder.lte
documentation:
A 'less than or equal to' filter

Args:
    column: The name of the column to apply a filter on
    value: The value to filter by

methods.name: postgrest.base_request_builder.BaseFilterRequestBuilder.is_

An 'is' filter

Args:
    column: The name of the column to apply a filter on
    value: The value to filter by

methods.name: postgrest.base_request_builder.BaseFilterRequestBuilder.like
documentation:
A 'LIKE' filter, to use for pattern matching.

Args:
    column: The name of the column to apply a filter on
    pattern: The pattern to filter by

methods.name: postgrest.base_request_builder.BaseFilterRequestBuilder.like_all_of
documentation:
A 'LIKE' filter, to use for pattern matching.

Args:
    column: The name of the column to apply a filter on
    pattern: The pattern to filter by

A 'LIKE' filter, to use for pattern matching.

Args:
    column: The name of the column to apply a filter on
    pattern: The pattern to filter by

methods.name: postgrest.base_request_builder.BaseFilterRequestBuilder.ilike_all_of
documentation:
A 'ILIKE' filter, to use for pattern matching (case insensitive).

Args:
    column: The name of the column to apply a filter on
    pattern: The pattern to filter by

methods.name: postgrest.base_request_builder.BaseFilterRequestBuilder.ilike_any_of
documentation:
A 'ILIKE' filter, to use for pattern matching (case insensitive).

Args:
    column: The name of the column to apply a filter on
    pattern: The pattern to filter by

methods.name: postgrest.base_request_builder.BaseFilterRequestBuilder.ilike
documentation:
An 'ILIKE' filter, to use for pattern matching (case insensitive).

Args:
    column: The name of the column to apply a filter on
    pattern: The pattern to filter by

methods.name: postgrest.base_request_builder.BaseFilterRequestBuilder.or_
documentation:
An 'or' filter

Args:
    filters: The filters to use, following PostgREST syntax
    reference_table: Set this to filter on referenced tables instead of the parent table

methods.name: postgrest.base_request_builder.BaseSelectRequestBuilder.order
documentation:
Sort the returned rows in some specific order.

Args:

: column: The column to order by
  desc: Whether the rows should be ordered in descending order or not.
  nullsfirst: nullsfirst
  foreign_table: Foreign table name whose results are to be ordered.

:::{versionchanged} 0.10.3 Allow ordering results for foreign tables with the foreign_table parameter.
:::

methods.name: postgrest.base_request_builder.BaseSelectRequestBuilder.limit
documentation:
Limit the number of rows returned by a query.

Args:

: size: The number of rows to be returned
  foreign_table: Foreign table name to limit

:::{versionchanged} 0.10.3 Allow limiting results returned for foreign tables with the foreign_table parameter.
:::

methods.name: postgrest.base_request_builder.BaseSelectRequestBuilder.offset
documentation:
Set the starting row index returned by a query.
Args:
    size: The number of the row to start at

methods.name: postgrest.base_request_builder.BaseRPCRequestBuilder.select
documentation:
Run a SELECT query.

Args:

: columns: The names of the columns to fetch.

Returns:

: {class}`BaseSelectRequestBuilder`

methods.name: postgrest.base_request_builder.BaseRPCRequestBuilder.single
documentation:
Specify that the query will only return a single row in response.

:::{caution}
The API will raise an error if the query returned more than one row.
:::

methods.name: postgrest.base_request_builder.BaseRPCRequestBuilder.maybe_single
documentation: Retrieves at most one row from the result. Result must be at most one row (e.g. using `eq` on a UNIQUE column), otherwise this will result in an error.

methods.name: postgrest.base_request_builder.BaseRPCRequestBuilder.csv
documentation: Specify that the query must retrieve data as a single CSV string.

Execute the query.

:::{tip}
This is the last method called, after the query is built.
:::

Returns:

Raises:

This is the last method called, after the query is built.
:::

: {class}`APIError` If the API raised an error.

methods.name: postgrest._async.request_builder.AsyncRequestBuilder.insert
documentation:
Run an INSERT query.

Args:

: json: The row to be inserted.
  count: The method to use to get the count of rows returned.
  returning: Either 'minimal' or 'representation'
  upsert: Whether the query should be an upsert.
  default_to_null: Make missing fields default to `null`.

  > Otherwise, use the default value for the column.
  > Only applies for bulk inserts.

Returns:

: {class}`AsyncQueryRequestBuilder`

methods.name: postgrest._async.request_builder.AsyncRequestBuilder.upsert
documentation:
Run an upsert (INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE) query.

Args:

: json: The row to be inserted.
  count: The method to use to get the count of rows returned.
  returning: Either 'minimal' or 'representation'
  ignore_duplicates: Whether duplicate rows should be ignored.
  on_conflict: Specified columns to be made to work with UNIQUE constraint.
  default_to_null: Make missing fields default to `null`. Otherwise, use the

  > default value for the column. This only applies when inserting new rows,
  > not when merging with existing rows under `ignoreDuplicates: false`.
  > This also only applies when doing bulk upserts.

Returns:

: {class}`AsyncQueryRequestBuilder`
